content.problemStatement:
A participant can make 111 submission every 303030 seconds. If a contest lasts for XXX minutes, what is the maximum number of submissions that the participant can make during it?

It is also given that the participant cannot make any submission in the last 555 seconds of the contest.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer XXX, denoting the number of minutes.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum number of submissions a participant can make in XXX minutes.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 30), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 30) – the duration of the contest in minutes.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the maximum number of submissions the participant can make.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each X, the maximum submissions equal 2·X. Hence 1→2, 2→4, 3→6, 4→8.
X=5 minutes → 2·5=10 submissions; X=10 minutes → 2·10=20 submissions.
Maximum allowed X is 30; answer is 2·30=60 submissions.

solution.approach: A submission can be made every 30 seconds, but no submission is allowed in the last 5 seconds of the contest. For a contest lasting X minutes, total seconds = 60·X. Usable seconds = 60·X − 5. The first possible submission can be at time 0, so the number of submissions is floor(usable/30) + 1. Simplifying: floor((60·X − 5)/30) + 1 = floor(2·X − 0.166…) + 1 = (2·X − 1) + 1 = 2·X. Hence the answer for each test case is simply 2·X.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
